req.title: Open checking account
req.statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-001 defines a bounded deposit range ($25–$50,000) for checking account creation, with a verified customer precondition. Test design applies Equivalence Partitioning (valid vs. invalid deposit ranges), Boundary Value Analysis (exact limits and off-by-one values), Negative Path testing (unverified customer, out-of-range deposits), and Decision Table analysis (verified status × deposit range). The suite ensures deterministic, repeatable validation of the account creation API endpoint with proper HTTP status codes (201/200 for success, 422 for validation error, 403 for authorization failure) and outcome verification (account created, notification dispatched).

tests.name: test_create_checking_account_valid_midrange_deposit
preconditions: no prior checking account exists for customer
expected: notification event published for welcome message
trace: R-FIN-001: expected outcome—account created + notification dispatched
tests.executable:
import requests
import json

def test_create_checking_account_valid_midrange_deposit():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    cust_id = '<setup_verified_customer_id>'
    payload = {
        'customer_id': cust_id,
        'account_type': 'checking',
        'initial_deposit': 1000
    }
    resp = requests.post(f'{base_url}/accounts/checking', json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f'Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}'
    data = resp.json()
    assert data.get('account_id'), 'account_id missing from response'
    assert data.get('status') == 'active', 'account status not active'
    assert data.get('initial_deposit') == 1000, 'deposit mismatch'
    assert '<notification_event_in_queue>', 'notification not dispatched'

tests.name: test_create_checking_account_reject_deposit_below_minimum
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_create_checking_account_reject_deposit_below_minimum():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    cust_id = '<setup_verified_customer_id>'
    payload = {
        'customer_id': cust_id,
        'account_type': 'checking',
        'initial_deposit': 24
    }
    resp = requests.post(f'{base_url}/accounts/checking', json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f'Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}'
    data = resp.json()
    assert 'error' in data, 'error field missing'
    assert 'deposit' in data.get('error', '').lower(), 'error message missing deposit reference'

tests.name: test_create_checking_account_reject_unverified_customer
tests.executable:
import requests

def test_create_checking_account_reject_unverified_customer():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    cust_id = '<setup_unverified_customer_id>'
    payload = {
        'customer_id': cust_id,
        'account_type': 'checking',
        'initial_deposit': 1000
    }
    resp = requests.post(f'{base_url}/accounts/checking', json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f'Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}'
    data = resp.json()
    assert 'error' in data, 'error field missing'
    assert 'verified' in data.get('error', '').lower(), 'error missing verification reference'

tests.name: test_create_checking_account_idempotency_duplicate_request
preconditions: no prior checking account exists for customer
actions: POST /accounts/checking with idempotency_key = UUID and deposit 2500
POST /accounts/checking with same idempotency_key and identical body
expected: second request: HTTP 201 Created or 409 Conflict (implementation-dependent)
only one account created in database
trace: R-FIN-001: state transition coverage—idempotent account creation
R-FIN-001: confirmed duplicate requests do not create duplicate accounts
tests.executable:
import requests
import uuid

def test_create_checking_account_idempotency_duplicate_request():
    base_url = 'http://localhost:8080'
    cust_id = '<setup_verified_customer_id>'
    idempotency_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        'customer_id': cust_id,
        'account_type': 'checking',
        'initial_deposit': 2500
    }
    headers = {'Idempotency-Key': idempotency_key}
    resp1 = requests.post(f'{base_url}/accounts/checking', json=payload, headers=headers)
    resp2 = requests.post(f'{base_url}/accounts/checking', json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp1.status_code == 201, f'First request failed: {resp1.status_code}'
    assert resp1.json().get('account_id') == resp2.json().get('account_id'), 'account_ids differ'
